diagrams.title: Earned Value Analysis Framework
diagrams.description: A comprehensive diagram showing the relationship between PV, EV, and AC and how variances and indices are calculated
elements: Planned Value (PV) - Budgeted cost of scheduled work
Earned Value (EV) - Budgeted cost of completed work
Actual Cost (AC) - Actual cost of completed work
Schedule Variance (SV) = EV - PV
Cost Variance (CV) = EV - AC
Schedule Performance Index (SPI) = EV/PV
Cost Performance Index (CPI) = EV/AC
EAC = BAC/CPI (performance-based)
ETC = EAC - AC (estimate to complete)
VAC = BAC - EAC (variance at completion)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how EVM integrates cost and schedule metrics to provide a unified view of project performance and forecast final outcomes

diagrams.title: ISO/IEC 25010 Quality Model
diagrams.description: Hierarchical structure of eight main quality characteristics and their sub-characteristics for software product evaluation
elements: Functional Suitability (Functional completeness, Functional correctness, Functional appropriateness)
Reliability (Maturity, Availability, Fault tolerance, Recoverability)
Usability (Appropriateness recognizability, Learnability, Operability, User error protection, User interface aesthetics, Accessibility)
Efficiency (Time behavior, Resource utilization, Capacity)
Maintainability (Modifiability, Analysability, Testability, Modularity, Reusability, Maintainability general)
Portability (Adaptability, Installability, Co-existence, Replaceability)
Security (Confidentiality, Integrity, Non-repudiation, Accountability, Authenticity)
Compatibility (Co-existence, Interoperability)
diagrams.purpose: Provide a standardized framework for evaluating software product quality across eight key dimensions, enabling objective comparison and quality assurance

diagrams.title: Project Health Check Dashboard
diagrams.description: Visual representation of project status using traffic light indicators across multiple dimensions
elements: Cost Status: Green (within 10% of budget), Yellow (10-20% over), Red (over 20%)
Schedule Status: Green (within 5% of plan), Yellow (5-15% behind), Red (over 15% behind)
Quality Status: Green (all tests passing), Yellow (minor defects), Red (major defects
Scope Status: Green (on scope), Yellow (minor scope creep), Red (significant scope changes)
Summary Box: Overall project health indicator
Action Items: List of required interventions for Yellow/Red items
diagrams.purpose: Provide stakeholders with immediate visual understanding of project health and areas requiring attention, facilitating timely decision-making
